title: Computer Networks
description: Introduction to computer networking covering the TCP/IP protocol stack, network architectures, and distributed applications. The course follows a top-down approach starting with application-layer protocols (HTTP, DNS, SMTP) and progressing through transport (TCP, UDP), network (IP, routing), and link layers. Students study socket programming, reliable data transfer, congestion control, routing algorithms, and network security fundamentals. Lab assignments involve implementing network protocols and applications using Python socket programming. The prerequisite CS201 provides the data structures knowledge needed for implementing networking algorithms and protocol state machines.
topics: Application layer: HTTP, DNS, SMTP, FTP
Network layer: IP addressing and subnetting
Data link layer and MAC protocols
outcomes: Explain the TCP/IP protocol stack and encapsulation
Design IP addressing schemes with subnetting
Compare routing algorithms and their convergence properties
Understand network security threats and countermeasures
